PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Jan-25-2023 Continued To Apr-26-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom: John Rueppel, Esq. and Azita Rahim, Esq. for Maria Elena Benavente (Petitioner). Sarah Brooks, Esq. for Thomas Harvey, Peter Harvey, and Douglas Harvey (Petitioners). Oliver Bray, Esq. for Mark Unger (Practice Administrator).

The Court stated that it had reviewed the supplemental joint case management statement and inquired about alternative dispute resolution (ADR). Mr. Rueppel stated that mediation is set for early March and that they are communicating and sharing documents. Ms. Brooks stated that they are at a bottleneck and that the practice administrator needs access to the electronics, passwords, etc. Mr. Bray stated that he doesn't think that they will need the Court's assistance, that they had a productive meeting yesterday, that there is a meeting next week with the forensic tech person, that things are moving slowly, and that they will know more next week. The Court stated that it thought that having a practice administrator in place that both parties were comfortable with would help the process and that it is surprised to hear about the practice administrator's need for the password, login, etc. The Court issued an advisory comment to counsel regarding the password issue, stated that the matter was on calendar today to set a trial date, and that it would prefer for mediation to take place prior to setting trial. Ms. Brooks addressed trial setting, stated that both of her clients are spending a lot of money, and noted that one of her clients isn't a beneficiary. The Court stated that it would continue the matter 90 days to allow time for the mediation to take place noting that at the next hearing it will be in a better place to set a trial date.

The Petition for Probate of Will and Letters Testamentary, the Petition for Probate of Will and Letters of Administrator with Will Annexed, and the Case Management Hearing are continued to April 26, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. In addition, the Court set status on mediation for April 26, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. A joint mediation statement is to be submitted one week prior to the hearing date.

At the request of Ms. Brooks, the Petition for Interpretation of Will, or in the alternative, Request for Reformation of Will is continued from February 27, 2023, to April 26, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Mr. Rueppel reports that Ms. Brooks and he have had preliminary discussions regarding the preceding. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to their competing petitions. Ms. Brooks suggests the court schedule an evidentiary hearing regarding the issue of whether the Statutory Will and Holographic Will constitute a single Will, admissible to probate. The court offers its position regarding an evidentiary hearing. Mr. Rueppel advises that the request for an evidentiary hearing is appropriate. The court advises counsel that at this point it sounds reasonable to continue this matter to allow for discovery and see where the matter is at the next calling before the court orders the parties to file another joint case management statement. Mr. Schultz reports on the progress that his client has made marshaling assets and personal property and that the sale of the estate's real property is proceeding. Mr. Rueppel advises that the parties have discussed the appointment of a practice administrator and asks if the court is amenable to appointing the administrator via stipulation and order. The court advises the parties to file an ex parte application to appoint a practice administrator, instead.

After additional discussion, all subject matters are continued to Oct-12-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. A Status on the filing of a Reformation Petition is set for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.
